channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: US agency adopts rule to make it easier for consumers to cancel unwanted subscriptions
item.description: The Federal Trade Commission adopted a final rule Wednesday that will require businesses to make it easy for consumers to cancel unwanted subscriptions and memberships.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Anthropic updates its risk governance framework
item.description: The launch this week by Anthropic of an update to its Responsible Scaling Policy (RSP), the risk governance framework it says it uses to “mitigate potential catastrophic risks from frontier AI systems,” is part of the company’s push to be perceived as an AI safety first provider compared to its comp
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: iPad mini 7 specs reveal 5 potentially annoying limitations
item.description: The iPad mini 7 is now official – and the tablet's specs reveal that Apple has cut a few corners to keep its price tag low.
item.dc:creator: mark.wilson@futurenet.com (Mark Wilson), TechRadar

item.title: How AI can unlock innovation across our nation’s research institutions
item.description: At a university campus in Rolla, Missouri (population 22,000), a small team of scientists is developing a glass powder that, when applied to wounds, significantly improves blood clotting and reduces risk of infection.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Leland, Fast Company

item.title: SpaceX blasts past 100 launches in 2024 with 101st from California
item.description: The aerospace titan does a lot of heavy lifting — and it's hard to believe that the aerospace giant has already launched more that 100 rockets this year alone.
item.dc:creator: Los Angeles Daily News, The Mercury News

item.title: Spain's Data Center Market Heats Up with Damac Group's 400 Million Euro Investment
item.description: Damac Group enters Spain's data center market with a 400 million euro investment, marking a significant milestone in the country's digital infrastructure development.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Cómo gestionar los programas de IA generativa: gobernanza, educación, regulación
item.description: La inteligencia artificial (IA) generativa ha crecido con gran rapidez, atrayendo inversiones y despertando el interés y la creatividad de los desarrolladores. Las empresas ven en la IA generativa una vía para crear servicios innovadores y revolucionar sus sectores.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft settles video gamers' lawsuit over Activision takeover
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Microsoft Corp. has settled a lawsuit from a group of gamers who sued to try to stop the company from buying video game publisher Activision Blizzard for $69 billion last year. Terms of the settlement were not disclosed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump says he likes Mark Zuckerberg 'much better now'
item.description: Former President Trump said he likes Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg “much better now,” as the head of Facebook and Instagram increasingly looks to stay out of politics.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: How to transform scientific discoveries into better treatments
item.description: As I reflect on how we can more effectively bring treatments to patients, one central question emerges: How can we transform scientific discoveries into better treatments? The intersection of science and innovation is rapidly evolving, yet translating cutting-edge research into impactful therapies r
item.dc:creator: Guadalupe Hayes-Mota, Fast Company

item.title: How to choose a flexible unified SASE solution
item.description: SASE is at the top of all CIOs’ minds, and for good reason. When deployed properly, SASE, which combines SD-WAN with cloud-delivered security via SSE, enables all users and devices, regardless of location, to securely access an organization’s hybrid network.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Journalist says FBI visited after he published hacked Vance document
item.description: An independent journalist says he was visited by the FBI after he published material allegedly obtained through an Iranian hack of former President Trump’s campaign.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: Voter beware: Text scam targets you, no matter where you live
item.description: Reports warn of a scam text falsely claiming that recipients aren’t registered to vote. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals what you need to know and five tips to avoid such schemes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Hong Kong zoo seeks answers after 9 monkeys die in 2 days
item.description: Hong Kong’s oldest zoo is seeking answers in a monkey medical mystery after nine animals died in two days, including three members of a critically endangered species.
item.dc:creator: Kanis Leung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Tech consultant goes on trial in death of Cash App founder Bob Lee
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — The tech consultant charged in Cash App founder Bob Lee's stabbing death had no motive to kill him and in fact was forced to defend himself against Lee, who had become aggressive while on a multiday drug bender, lawyers for Nima Momeni said in opening statements Monday.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The Future of KOSA: House Speaker Mike Johnson's Uncertain Stance on Online Safety
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson's comments on the Kids Online Safety Act (KOSA) have left its future uncertain, sparking concerns among advocates for online privacy and safety for children.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Open source package entry points could be used for command jacking
item.description: Open source application packages, including those in Python and JavaScript, have a vulnerability in their entry points that could be used by threat actors to execute malicious code to steal data, plant malware, and more.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: Making generative AI work for you
item.description: It’s not often someone can talk about genAI in a “pragmatic and realistic” way, but those are exactly the accolades handed out to AWS Product Management Director Massimo Re Ferrè following his recent generative AI (genAI) talk. It’s not hard to find opposing sentiment.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Top 5 methods to equip your car with Wi-Fi
item.description: Having Wi-Fi has become a necessity for many of us. Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explores the top five ways to equip your vehicles with Wi-Fi.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Over 77,000 customers' personal information is exposed in Fidelity Investments data breach
item.description: Fidelity Investments, one of the world's biggest asset managers, experienced a data breach in August, the company's second in 12 months.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Connect in a crisis with iOS 18's new video call feature for emergency services
item.description: Apple’s new Emergency SOS Live Video feature allows users to share live video footage of the emergency directly with first responders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ktor 3.0 switches to kotlinx.io library
item.description: Ktor 3.0, the latest version of the Kotlin-based framework for building asynchronous client- and server-side applications, is now available. Ktor 3.0 switches to the kotlinx.io library for handling data sources. The release also highlights support for server-sent events (SSE).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AMD unveils new generation of Epyc, Instinct chips
item.description: AMD announced its latest AI and high-performance computing processors at its Advancing AI event in San Francisco, including the fifth generation of its Epyc server processors and AMD Instinct MI325X accelerators.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Pentagon releases final CMMC rule, paving way for implementation
item.description: The Pentagon has taken another big step forward to make CMMC a reality. DoD projects the requirements will start showing up in contracts by mid-2025. The post Pentagon releases final CMMC rule, paving way for implementation first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Scammers exploit grief with fake funeral streaming on Facebook
item.description: Scammers are offering video streaming for funeral services and taking advantage of people who are grieving to get credit card information.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
